name: fortyFiveCushionBounce
description: 45-degree approach to the top rail: normal component reverses scaled by cushion restitution 0.75, tangential component scaled by retention 0.7. Retention < restitution makes the rebound STEEPER than the mirror (43.0 degrees from the rail normal instead of 45) - real banks play short (Alciatore, billiards.colostate.edu bank-shot analysis).
derivation: Path from (-0.9, 0) along (1,1)/sqrt2 meets the reflection line y = 0.635 - r = 0.606425 after t = 0.606425*sqrt2 ~= 0.857614 m at (-0.293575, 0.606425).
Rail model: vx -> 0.7*vx (tangential retention f), vy -> -0.75*vy (normal COR e): v_out = (0.519697, -0.556818), exit direction (14, -15)/sqrt(421).
Exit angle from the rail normal: atan(0.7/0.75) ~= 43.025 deg < 45 deg mirror - the bank shortens by ~2 deg because f < e.
(Numbers in the JSON are exact IEEE doubles; the commentary rounds to 6 dp.)
